body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/67309227/419299223/-630682683?amount=15&source=EM_NBI_DK_20230730_FR_1.5_0_6MO_campaign_NA_NA_EOM&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83Nzg3NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNDQ1ZGZmNS0xMzJmLWVlMTEtYjhmMC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UvXPByY4HIcjVIy0kVc3CybSqBo11MSK1oi49cveChw=)
Dan’s in Congress to advocate for working families and fight for Michigan. He has shown time and time again that he’ll work with anyone — Republicans, Democrats, and Independents — to:
* Cap the cost of insulin and lower the cost of prescription drugs
* Preserve Social Security and Medicare
* Protect abortion rights as extreme Republicans increase their fight to criminalize abortion and ban birth control
* Bring good-paying manufacturing jobs, better infrastructure, affordable health care, and clean water

_But too many of Dan’s colleagues are not interested in that. And now, extreme national Republicans are attacking Dan for standing up for reproductive rights for women in the military._

The Republican-led House is not serious about governing. Rather than passing serious proposals meant to help Americans, they keep prioritizing extremist measures to score political points. **The House GOP’s failure to stand up to their most extreme voices is why it is so crucial we take back the House next year.

But we can’t do that without winning this district. We need to raise $3,900 more online by the end of this month to fight back against the attacks against Dan and set the record straight.

_[The path to regaining the majority in the House runs right through MI-08. Will you help us prove we have the strength to do it? Stand with Dan before our big deadline tomorrow night:](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/67309228/419299224/-630682683?amount=15&source=EM_NBI_DK_20230730_FR_1.5_0_6MO_campaign_NA_NA_EOM&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83Nzg3NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNDQ1ZGZmNS0xMzJmLWVlMTEtYjhmMC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UvXPByY4HIcjVIy0kVc3CybSqBo11MSK1oi49cveChw=)_**

Extremism and polarization only serve to block progress. name: Team Bennet
subject: "trickle down"
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BENNET FOR COLORADO
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/embutton?refcode=X_073023_EOM_A_Boe_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/embutton?refcode=X_073023_EOM_A_Boe_act]
Hi friend –
Every day, Michael goes to work for the people of Colorado in the Senate — for our families, our seniors, and our kids. He fights for big-impact issues that hit close home.
One of his top priorities has been the expanded Child Tax Credit, which would put money back in the pockets of working parents. If passed, this bill would benefit 1 million children in Colorado — and 60 million across the country.
We live in one of the wealthiest countries in the world, but we still have one of the highest rates of childhood poverty. With Michael’s leadership, we can change that.
Unfortunately, not every Congressperson from Colorado has the same values.
Lauren Boebert, the Republican representing Colorado's third congressional district, has repeatedly voted to cut taxes for the richest people in this country. Meanwhile, working parents across our state are struggling to make rent and get food on the table.
Cutting taxes at the very top doesn’t lead to a “trickle down” effect for the rest of us. That’s why Michael is working on solutions that connect directly to kids and families.
Michael is fighting for our interests in the Senate and is working hard to elect and re-elect pro-choice, pro-democracy leaders, who deliver for their constituents. Unlike Lauren Boebert.
Michael doesn’t take a dime from corporate PACs and federal lobbyists— which is why grassroots support is so important. Will you chip in now so we can hit our monthly fundraising goal before Monday’s critical end-of-month deadline? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/embutton?refcode=X_073023_EOM_A_Boe_act]

subject: Summer vacation
body:
and our Q3 deadline |
---

| | | | Our first deadline of the quarter is tomorrow, and **[_I’d appreciate any amount you can contribute._](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/67310296/419301628/-126703183?refcode=folf230730&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MjY3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZTQ3Y2VhZS0xNDJmLWVlMTEtYjhmMC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=C3-mBUARkYnkT0Rzsv19JRT5t7-L8162O6FBG6gHfHQ=)** Typically, campaigns see a slowdown in fundraising during the summer as people take vacations or time off. We get it! But with everything going on, we can’t stop. And I know #TeamLizzie is incredibly dedicated, and together, we can keep up the awesome energy we’ve had this whole year and hit these important fundraising benchmarks. Frankly, there isn’t any other option. We need to be prepared to run a strong campaign in 2024 and reaching our goals makes it possible. And this is a big election: Lizzie has three primary challengers, control of the House and Senate is up for grabs, and it’s a presidential election year. [_**That means we’ll need to hold this seat, help defend others, and pick up five more to win back the House. Any amount you can spare before our deadline tomorrow really would make a difference.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/67310298/419301629/-126703183?refcode=folf230730&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MjY3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZTQ3Y2VhZS0xNDJmLWVlMTEtYjhmMC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=C3-mBUARkYnkT0Rzsv19JRT5t7-L8162O6FBG6gHfHQ=) Talk soon, Collin

From efforts to throw citizens off voter rolls to outrageous conspiracy theories about the 2020 election, far-right extremists are doing everything they can to erode faith in our democratic institutions. The stakes couldn’t be any higher – and we can’t afford to back down. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | = | = | | | = | = | | | = | = The fact is, in the next election, many of the same people who tried to overturn the 2020 election are attempting to win back power. And if they succeed, they’ll be newly emboldened to ramp up their attacks on our democracy. From his time as Attorney General standing up to the Big Lie, to his work as Governor defending the right to vote, Josh is fighting day and night to secure our democracy – but these are tough fights, and none of us can afford to get complacent. = Thank you, Team Shapiro | | | = Text JOSH to 32210 By participating, you agree to the for recurring autodialed marketing messages from Josh Shapiro to the phone number you provide. body:
| | | Peter, this is Jackie with Rick Scott for Florida.

I just left a meeting where we talked through the top Senate battlegrounds, and I wanted to share what we discussed.

**Overall, these races are going to be tough, but with your support, we WILL take back a Republican Majority in the Senate and we WILL rescue our country from its left-leaning trajectory.**

I wanted to highlight something that everyone agreed on and that is how important it is to win Florida. You see, Florida is the Democrat's BIG prize, and they’d like nothing more than to see it turn blue.

_**The last time Republican Senator Rick Scott ran, it went to a recount[](http://links.voterickscott.com/u/click?_t=24d1692d4ad14657a18af9ad6ea13243&_m=2bdb53fd1c314e8c8ce158255bdbc54e&_e=ShwwzjVfhINi-OrhXioQUN5xGR81omQNGzrIVUVlY4nZBP8BN52We_bgEMcX9o27KkIT9BqiI2H9BeXFxg-ixT6bpFeRBU8yyu76Q8E6El3kfKedPXmMRG4x7leR3udrrhn1lSubHhzwk-62S4XWx9sgU47SngGMA6lw46aVLJ9vak8_g3ey5imp0YWNChNJ81DwSKDV4apQPg4rLYp9BLqiDNBY1KF6k_K5IP5vLJoU_ir8lSuWIrAa4xEnbX9E7FK97GaMCM6XxsDwVuPlbNCWT3xhqslzJRlXlqiMt40p7Q-n7ddaWx-_JfrjfRu5blsgfE4fUBUOZGh_kh8_yh1HhayRCfQ0kOZJ8qJER8w%3D), and he won by LESS THAN 1%. **_

subject: Less than 6,000 votes each
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Arizona Democrats**
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/67312329/419302826/-1397648958?refcode=AU_AZD_FR_GEN_EOM_AC_20230729_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS8xMDU1MTYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiODFmN2U3MzAtMTYyZi1lZTExLWI4ZjAtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=QPNdgbqfvwPisKWFoRpu5V4XfIFErcOKyfLTGGRy1hI=)
---
Here are a few things you should know, team: 📍 Both David Schweikert and Juan Ciscomani are in toss-up districts, meaning we have a real chance at flipping both AZ-1 and AZ-6 blue and taking back the House in 2024. 🗳️ Last election, each race was decided by less than **6,000 votes. ** 🌵 No one in the nation has margins that are closer than we do. It’s truly anyone’s game, and the work we do today will be the foundation to our success in 2024. | | | Bumping this important message for you. **_[Click here to donate to take a stand before our crucial deadline at midnight tomorrow→](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953ywjAHk5iaSyuXGJuhV0BPwa_xKTDsopFvjs3kbm8iG6H0ACfrzvu0uX6G2_-jA06bgOKrT9sp9W-IdBQ3GTfDdOPMRlRQjd5ewir8JPhx8YFYp1Q7qqWBXchNCquPXU-y_jHKbJdXubwaw7QpPCqThWB1ZOVLdYDY9q_tcplGE86C_0xH5svol7_RE0GeK3TKTA81kdCxvAh-MyXAtXSNefIIYnYMvIWmmA2rDiLhzXyPUOvH0x35pr1a0rUlpN24a_KNaSV-PPF8yvV7Ry_rg_Mblky1vd4NPK8rqxSTNj0pXlfit-T4AFIGsrX0SArI/3yb/ts1FzwA6T7uAPVlgGp_QUg/h0/i5XLAJ7nQFOCyv2YCEM4fW17uq1DjLgzdfCw7BY31TQ)_**
* * *
_**Original Message From Renee:**_

Here’s the thingR: I don’t have kids, and my husband and I have made a conscious decision not to have kids.

You’re probably asking yourself why I co-founded an organization that is entirely focused on kids and improving their future.

Let me explain: While I don’t plan to have kids, I still care about the direction this country is heading. Kids will inherit a world where the climate is in crisis, democracy is crumbling, and gun violence ends countless lives.

**And I believe they deserve better than that. It’s up to the current generation to make it better for them— _it’s our responsibility to take action._**

These kids will eventually be our doctors, lawyers, teachers, police officers and lawmakers, and we owe it to them to leave this place better than we found it. It doesn’t take a parent to understand the urgency.

The adage goes “It takes a village to raise a child”. It takes our collective effort to make sure that every child’s future is protected.

**_[That’s why I am asking you—whether you have children, want children, are content being the favorite aunt or uncle, or can do without—to care enough about the future to join me in the fight. Will you chip in $10 before our end-of-month fundraising deadline in 3 days to take a stand?](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953ywjAHk5iaSyuXGJuhV0BPwa_xKTDsopFvjs3kbm8iG6H0ACfrzvu0uX6G2_-jA06bgOKrT9sp9W-IdBQ3GTfDdOPMRlRQjd5ewir8JPhx8YFYp1Q7qqWBXchNCquPXU-xH4k1FLEvGC9AkC3UmuGf1Eu9XV99mKVNj08YxUpCoIZfYe-FzvapIaRQOlPhmNxb5GYWQeHHJlx9A9lyqHnz3oDBvrrWSz6f7kh5vnCT5rMo5rcT620Bpz5J0sEdgVq_2sNJKrLenqwU7CAK5MktIa8-lnlrglTQyvfdIrMPNauu646LjCv6aeyOQ9gCZmH8/3yb/ts1FzwA6T7uAPVlgGp_QUg/h1/SI09LzMjtfgkyvbdN0P3JG5MoSqS5-CvuObshmxkqv0)_**

name: Alan Dershowitz
subject: The Sun's re-emergence is a gift
body:
In over sixty years in the legal profession, I’ve experienced first hand the impact of both good journalism and bad journalism — mostly the latter — and just how important and rare it is to get things right. | | FROM THE DESK OF ALAN DERSHOWITZ
---
| | Dear Sun Reader,
---
| | In over sixty years in the legal profession, I’ve experienced first hand the impact of both good journalism and bad journalism — mostly the latter — and just how important and rare it is to get things right.

In today’s media landscape there are far too many publications that get things wrong. Many are more concerned with the political implications of a story than the basic facts.

The New York Sun is different. It’s a paper that places principle over politics and people over party.

Its in-depth coverage of the legal challenges faced by President Trump serve as a case in point.

When I defended President Trump during his first impeachment, I faced an avalanche of criticism, but few papers made sufficient effort to fully appreciate the significance of the legal arguments.

The Sun’s deeply knowledgeable leadership, including its editor Seth Lipsky and associate editor A.R. Hoffman are themselves students of the Constitution. It shows in their coverage, which I find to be first rate.

It will be no secret to you that I don’t agree with everything that can be found in the Sun’s pages, but I can say with confidence that its re-emergence is among the most important recent developments in American journalism.

I’ve been impressed by the Sun’s growth so far, but I know it faces an uphill battle, one that I have pledged to join.

The Sun’s success will be a gift to the American people and to the cause of truth. But to succeed, we need you to join us. I highly encourage you to join the Sun today.

Every person matters in this effort.

Don’t wait. The battle to restore American journalism starts now.
---
| | [ Join the Sun Now → ](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=17e4f0e38b11449386d0c54bad944af5&_e=UeFimvj6fr2EqeJgPJMGjOy0P22TMIiNUJJ8IjCcnxHsjV-5XDTUD_YNaUQr1V0Ta4GYjHEISu1q3qZMnUX7ghljR83vXyzWnMQABEJYobxguwLay1giVAxc-aZHvh0Nv-n-9GX17NqEXqbNB9KB03eyjFJIo96vAqxSU2fdFXPkkweg_bdgwdoOpiquNqDfmpN2LdwOQMXjDEuv7cD52UlHBHFxGZNjshUX_acFZ1ssmGIRbl2-XNI3n-3VH1lDF83gnOZ4zP7uY-IMiSbGe8d-fk6jxXaoET0GYg2fIxAbxrdhRncC3OvTCARwgLW1)
---
| | |
---
Alan Dershowitz
Columnist,
The New York Sun

Friend, we just pulled the numbers, and this is bad news:**Kirsten has less than 48 hours to hit her $50,000 end-of-month goal, but she's only 54% of the way there.**
Kirsten cannot afford to miss a goal as important as this one – especially given the enormous stakes of this election cycle. Last year, New York Republicans built a red wave, flipping enough seats to win back the House and very nearly winning the governorship. Now, they're more emboldened than ever before.
It's up to Kirsten and this grassroots team to lead a strong campaign at the top of the ticket so that we can flip those House seats back to blue, defend this must-win Senate seat and _stop_ another red wave.
**This race is a battle for the momentum advantage. And the GOP megadonors conspiring to defeat Kirsten aren't taking a single day off. By now, almost all of Mississippi is privy to the state's legislative measures to halt the University of Mississippi Medical Center's (UMMC) gender transition program. Emails that were captured through a public record's request reveal why UMMC chose to end the program. Simply put, lawmakers were angered that the hospital was working on such a vile attempt to harm children. The probe into UMMC'S Center for Gender and Sexual Minority Health began last August. Hormone therapy was being administered to minors. Kristy Simms, the spokesperson for UMMC to elected officials at state and federal levels, saw the writing on the wall when she emailed Dr. LouAnn Woodward, Vice Chancellor, on Sept. 12 of last year. Simms knew that either they should pause some- or all of the work, or they inevitably would be told to do so legislatively.
Other states have seen a rise in gender and hormone therapy and have taken legislative measures to stop it. In February of 2023, Mississippi Governor, Tate Reeves, signed a bill banning gender dysphoria hormone therapy for minors under 18. This was a bold and necessary move. Just about the time Mississippians sat down from that particular battle, we quickly realized there were still critical and urgent battles to be fought because the data harboring systems in place through hospitals were still working to undermine not only state laws regarding minors, but to deconstruct and dismantle the first institution designed by God - the family.
Recently, an alarming story of a central Mississippi family was brought to the attention of several Mississippi state legislators and other grassroots advocates regarding possible loopholes for vulnerable minors. The story you are about to read is a first-hand account detailing an egregiously scary incident that happened to a mother and son that left her very disturbed.
"My son began receiving care at UMMC in April due to sudden onset neurological symptoms. I have a personal MyChart under my name. When he became a patient, I was able to access his records at UMMC through MyChart by proxy account created in his name. All our testing and scheduling has been accessible to me through MyChart.
On Thursday, June 22, I took him to UMMC for a follow-up visit with his neurologist. Labs were drawn on this date. I was perplexed when I did not get any notifications of lab results coming in later that afternoon. On the evening of June 22nd, I sent a message through MyChart to his physician asking why I had lost access to his labs as I could not even find the icon to check them through MyChart. He responded that he did not know why I had lost access as he had changed nothing in the computer and asked me to reach out to MyChart helpline.
On Friday, June 23rd, I reached out to the help number given to me by my child's specialist. I was informed by the attendant that I had lost access to his labs and test results because he turned 12 on June 21st. The attendant told me I had lost access to protect his privacy due to HIPAA. When I pushed back, she stated that I could file an appeal. I continued to push back stating that he is a minor child, and if I am not taking care of his medical needs, who is? After some back-and-forth, she allowed me to have my son get on the phone and request a personal username and password to be sent to my email address.
I was then required to create a new account under his name where all tests/ lab results can now be viewed. They are still not accessible to me through my MyChart account. This is not, however, true of other organizations associated with our account. On Saturday, June 24th, I sent a message through MyChart requesting the specific HIPAA section that necessitated this change. I also asked them to provide me with any information regarding federal programs associated with this policy. I have still not received any correspondence back in response to my message.
My husband and I both are from medical backgrounds. My husband is a surgeon, and I am a speech pathologist. We are aware of what is normal and what is not normal in the medical world. This is not normal, and it is not acceptable. My fear is for unsuspecting parents who do not know their rights, and we are very concerned for rebellious and vulnerable children who can do dangerous things to themselves medically and keep important information from their parents. We feel fortunate that our child is not in rebellion, and that we were finally able to get access to his medical records, but this should never have happened with our minor son. Our hearts hurt for parents who are not experiencing the same closeness of relationship with their children because this can have catastrophic consequences. We feel this increases their vulnerability in the system. We also have other children to raise and may find ourselves in a precarious spot in the future. It has also been very difficult for us to manage care for our son, juggling two different MyChart accounts. It breaks down the contiguous nature of his medical records and our access to them within one system."
If the nature of this recent story does not send chills down your spine and anger you, then maybe you need a Biblical and Constitutional pulse check. This is outrageous! These systems are used all around the nation, and this particular story comes on the heels of many states, like California and New York, dismembering inalienable, parental rights through tyrannical legislative means. They believe parents should have no authority over their children and that the children are a product of the government.
Mississippi House Representative Becky Currie proposed legislation in the 2022-2023 session that would protect parents and their inalienable rights over their children. There was not enough support in the Republican supermajority seated in the heart of the Bible Belt. Why? Will there be enough bicameral interest this coming session to batten down the hatches and push back on sick perversions against our children and our families and any and every loophole the enemy would use to subvert the most important institution created? Will there be enough common sense to support and uphold the Higher Moral law of God? Will the citizens of each county begin to hold their elected representative voices accountable to protect our most vulnerable populations?
If you are a parent of a minor who has an affiliation with UMMC's pediatric group, you might want to, as the parent or guardian, request all their medical records before they turn twelve years of age. If you do not, you just might be asking permission from your child, who is yet to earn a license, hold a job, or earn a paycheck, to give consent to these rogue institutions for you to be able to see their medical charts or enter a clinical room setting with them. Hell will freeze over before that happens to my family.
On defense, UMMC issued a generic statement regarding the MyChart shady business and posted it on social media. They insinuated that they are not keeping parents from accessing their child's records. I call "bull," and many of you do, too. Too many parents have had the same issues with not being able to access their child's medical records, and we are hearing from them. How many parents missed the memo, so to speak, and would not realize that they no longer have parental authority? These outlets of corporate communism and usurpations are despotically designed by systemically cancerous ideological minds to "capture the children." That should quicken every red-blooded American to the front lines to defend and preserve our most precious asset: Our heritage. Please come out from under your rock.
It does seem that MyChart can be set up how organizations need it to be set up within their system. _Why would this hospital set up MyChart in this manner?_ The question is not so much raised with MyChart but directed at UMMC. When researching Mississippi state law regarding minors and consent, Title X laws are glaring. Could this be the loophole for UMMC making it more difficult for parents to have access to the full medical charts of _their_ children? If a minor is seeking services through Title X, providers must allow minors to obtain Title X services on their own consent, even if state law requires parental consent or notification.
Mississippi state law also allows a physician or a psychologist to provide consultation or medication to a child for mental or emotional problems related to drugs and alcohol without parental consent. Minors can consent to these services, and the law does not require the provider to inform the parents. Simply put, Title X services are confidential. Is the University of Mississippi Medical Center using state and Title X laws to circumvent parental knowledge and advocacy regarding not only STDs, drug use, contraceptives, and pregnancy, but a gamut of other medical conditions, discussions, and treatment? We must begin to dig and ask the critical questions given the global, national, and state agendas to completely breakdown the family units and capture the children.
The Title X government funded program was enacted in 1970 as part of a Public Health Service Act. While the focus has been stated to be on contraceptives for teens, this project has many regulatory strongholds and touts, _" a broad range of acceptable and effective medically approving family methods...including services for adolescents."_ Read that again. Do you see the potentially catastrophic fallout and loophole that could undermine the family institution in a tangible way? _What could possibly go wrong?_ Dig into the HIPAA laws on parental consent. You will see that while parents are responsible for the care of their minor, and that it is a general rule that parents are entitled to know their minor's health information, there are exceptions. Under HIPAA, a parent may not be considered a minor child's personal representative and advocate if the child independently consented to a health care service, no other consent is required by law, and the child has not personally requested the parent to be the personal representative.
It is up to We the Parents to be the first and last line of defense for our posterity. Be educated. Be intentional about defending and preserving Biblical and Constitutional precepts. Hold legislative officials accountable to do the right things as an elected representative of your family, your voice, and your values.
If you or anyone you know has had a similar experience, please reach out to me or Representative Dana Criswell, Chairman of the Mississippi House Freedom Caucus, and share your story. ([DCriswell@house.ms.gov](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/43bb5f75-148d-408f-a499-0b73c48078cb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) We are not standing idly by while sinister agendas lay actively lurking to capture our children.
_Rebecca Chaney is the Director for Restore Liberty Mississippi, and she sits on the board for the Mississippi Freedom Caucus. She is a lifetime Patriot Academy Constitution Coach and has hosted Biblical Citizenship and Constitutional classes since 2020. She has helped to start these classes all across the state of Mississippi. She is a homeschool Mom of seven years to her son and daughter. You can read more of her work at_[https://substack.com/@rebeccachaney](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/37993582-331c-4b53-bc6e-54dacf56ca9e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) _and_[www.restore-liberty.org](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/c9b3f708-d288-49c8-9296-31e38d5c2d7f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

clean_body: | | ---|---|--- | | | | | cross-posted a post from ---|---|--- | | | Dana CriswellJul 30 * Dana Criswell ---|---|--- More great information from Rebecca. Parents must stand up and demand their God-given rights to rear their children. Government has no place in this relationship. A Sinister Game Mississippi Hospital Plays to Cheat Your Parental Win | | | Jul 30 | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- By now, almost all of Mississippi is privy to the state's legislative measures to halt the University of Mississippi Medical Center's (UMMC) gender transition program. Emails that were captured through a public record's request reveal why UMMC chose to end the program. Simply put, lawmakers were angered that the hospital was working on such a vile attempt to harm children. The probe into UMMC'S Center for Gender and Sexual Minority Health began last August. Hormone therapy was being administered to minors. Kristy Simms, the spokesperson for UMMC to elected officials at state and federal levels, saw the writing on the wall when she emailed Dr. LouAnn Woodward, Vice Chancellor, on Sept. 12 of last year. Simms knew that either they should pause some- or all of the work, or they inevitably would be told to do so legislatively. Other states have seen a rise in gender and hormone therapy and have taken legislative measures to stop it. In February of 2023, Mississippi Governor, Tate Reeves, signed a bill banning gender dysphoria hormone therapy for minors under 18. This was a bold and necessary move. Just about the time Mississippians sat down from that particular battle, we quickly realized there were still critical and urgent battles to be fought because the data harboring systems in place through hospitals were still working to undermine not only state laws regarding minors, but to deconstruct and dismantle the first institution designed by God - the family. Recently, an alarming story of a central Mississippi family was brought to the attention of several Mississippi state legislators and other grassroots advocates regarding possible loopholes for vulnerable minors. The story you are about to read is a first-hand account detailing an egregiously scary incident that happened to a mother and son that left her very disturbed. "My son began receiving care at UMMC in April due to sudden onset neurological symptoms. I have a personal MyChart under my name. When he became a patient, I was able to access his records at UMMC through MyChart by proxy account created in his name. All our testing and scheduling has been accessible to me through MyChart. On Thursday, June 22, I took him to UMMC for a follow-up visit with his neurologist. Labs were drawn on this date. I was perplexed when I did not get any notifications of lab results coming in later that afternoon. On the evening of June 22nd, I sent a message through MyChart to his physician asking why I had lost access to his labs as I could not even find the icon to check them through MyChart. He responded that he did not know why I had lost access as he had changed nothing in the computer and asked me to reach out to MyChart helpline. On Friday, June 23rd, I reached out to the help number given to me by my child's specialist. I was informed by the attendant that I had lost access to his labs and test results because he turned 12 on June 21st. The attendant told me I had lost access to protect his privacy due to HIPAA. When I pushed back, she stated that I could file an appeal. I continued to push back stating that he is a minor child, and if I am not taking care of his medical needs, who is? After some back-and-forth, she allowed me to have my son get on the phone and request a personal username and password to be sent to my email address. I was then required to create a new account under his name where all tests/ lab results can now be viewed. They are still not accessible to me through my MyChart account. This is not, however, true of other organizations associated with our account. On Saturday, June 24th, I sent a message through MyChart requesting the specific HIPAA section that necessitated this change. I also asked them to provide me with any information regarding federal programs associated with this policy. I have still not received any correspondence back in response to my message. My husband and I both are from medical backgrounds. My husband is a surgeon, and I am a speech pathologist. We are aware of what is normal and what is not normal in the medical world. This is not normal, and it is not acceptable. My fear is for unsuspecting parents who do not know their rights, and we are very concerned for rebellious and vulnerable children who can do dangerous things to themselves medically and keep important information from their parents. We feel fortunate that our child is not in rebellion, and that we were finally able to get access to his medical records, but this should never have happened with our minor son. Our hearts hurt for parents who are not experiencing the same closeness of relationship with their children because this can have catastrophic consequences. We feel this increases their vulnerability in the system. We also have other children to raise and may find ourselves in a precarious spot in the future. It has also been very difficult for us to manage care for our son, juggling two different MyChart accounts. It breaks down the contiguous nature of his medical records and our access to them within one system." If the nature of this recent story does not send chills down your spine and anger you, then maybe you need a Biblical and Constitutional pulse check. This is outrageous! These systems are used all around the nation, and this particular story comes on the heels of many states, like California and New York, dismembering inalienable, parental rights through tyrannical legislative means. They believe parents should have no authority over their children and that the children are a product of the government. Mississippi House Representative Becky Currie proposed legislation in the 2022-2023 session that would protect parents and their inalienable rights over their children. There was not enough support in the Republican supermajority seated in the heart of the Bible Belt. Why? Will there be enough bicameral interest this coming session to batten down the hatches and push back on sick perversions against our children and our families and any and every loophole the enemy would use to subvert the most important institution created? Will there be enough common sense to support and uphold the Higher Moral law of God? Will the citizens of each county begin to hold their elected representative voices accountable to protect our most vulnerable populations? If you are a parent of a minor who has an affiliation with UMMC's pediatric group, you might want to, as the parent or guardian, request all their medical records before they turn twelve years of age. If you do not, you just might be asking permission from your child, who is yet to earn a license, hold a job, or earn a paycheck, to give consent to these rogue institutions for you to be able to see their medical charts or enter a clinical room setting with them. Hell will freeze over before that happens to my family. On defense, UMMC issued a generic statement regarding the MyChart shady business and posted it on social media. They insinuated that they are not keeping parents from accessing their child's records. I call "bull," and many of you do, too. Too many parents have had the same issues with not being able to access their child's medical records, and we are hearing from them. How many parents missed the memo, so to speak, and would not realize that they no longer have parental authority? These outlets of corporate communism and usurpations are despotically designed by systemically cancerous ideological minds to "capture the children." That should quicken every red-blooded American to the front lines to defend and preserve our most precious asset: Our heritage. Please come out from under your rock. It does seem that MyChart can be set up how organizations need it to be set up within their system. Why would this hospital set up MyChart in this manner? The question is not so much raised with MyChart but directed at UMMC. When researching Mississippi state law regarding minors and consent, Title X laws are glaring. Could this be the loophole for UMMC making it more difficult for parents to have access to the full medical charts of their children? If a minor is seeking services through Title X, providers must allow minors to obtain Title X services on their own consent, even if state law requires parental consent or notification. Mississippi state law also allows a physician or a psychologist to provide consultation or medication to a child for mental or emotional problems related to drugs and alcohol without parental consent. Minors can consent to these services, and the law does not require the provider to inform the parents. Simply put, Title X services are confidential. Is the University of Mississippi Medical Center using state and Title X laws to circumvent parental knowledge and advocacy regarding not only STDs, drug use, contraceptives, and pregnancy, but a gamut of other medical conditions, discussions, and treatment? We must begin to dig and ask the critical questions given the global, national, and state agendas to completely breakdown the family units and capture the children. The Title X government funded program was enacted in 1970 as part of a Public Health Service Act. While the focus has been stated to be on contraceptives for teens, this project has many regulatory strongholds and touts, " a broad range of acceptable and effective medically approving family methods...including services for adolescents." Read that again. Do you see the potentially catastrophic fallout and loophole that could undermine the family institution in a tangible way? What could possibly go wrong? Dig into the HIPAA laws on parental consent. You will see that while parents are responsible for the care of their minor, and that it is a general rule that parents are entitled to know their minor's health information, there are exceptions. Under HIPAA, a parent may not be considered a minor child's personal representative and advocate if the child independently consented to a health care service, no other consent is required by law, and the child has not personally requested the parent to be the personal representative. It is up to We the Parents to be the first and last line of defense for our posterity. Be educated. Be intentional about defending and preserving Biblical and Constitutional precepts. Hold legislative officials accountable to do the right things as an elected representative of your family, your voice, and your values. If you or anyone you know has had a similar experience, please reach out to me or Representative Dana Criswell, Chairman of the Mississippi House Freedom Caucus, and share your story. () We are not standing idly by while sinister agendas lay actively lurking to capture our children. Rebecca Chaney is the Director for Restore Liberty Mississippi, and she sits on the board for the Mississippi Freedom Caucus. She is a lifetime Patriot Academy Constitution Coach and has hosted Biblical Citizenship and Constitutional classes since 2020. She has helped to start these classes all across the state of Mississippi. She is a homeschool Mom of seven years to her son and daughter. You can read more of her work at and | | | | | | | | Originally posted on | | | | Yarborough Chaney ---|---|--- | ? Civil Stewardship from a Biblical Worldview (C) 2023 Dana Criswell 530 George St., Suite 200, Jackson, MS ? ?

body:
State officials finally allow abortion rights petition to proceed | | | |
---
| | |
# Capitol Report
July 30, 2023
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| **Contact Senator Roberts** 201 W. Capitol Ave., Rm 329
Jefferson City, MO 65101
(573) 751-4415
Steve.Roberts@senate.mo.gov
---
| | | | | | [](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=dc14b826a7&e=a088917f89)
---
| | [](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=950930d530&e=a088917f89)
---
| | [](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=ec70cbfe68&e=a088917f89)
---
|
---
| **State officials finally allow abortion rights petition to proceed**
State officials on July 26 finally signed off an initiative effort that seeks to put a proposed constitutional amendment protecting abortion rights on next year’s statewide ballot. The final certification of the petitions came nearly two months after the May 1 statutory deadline by which the job was supposed to have been completed due to procedural obstruction by Republican officeholders, with further delays still looming.

The Missouri Supreme Court eliminated one obstacle on July 20 when it ruled Attorney General Andrew Bailey had no legal authority to try to force State Treasurer Scott Fitzpatrick to drastically inflate the estimated cost to taxpayers of enshrining abortion rights in the state constitution. Bailey and Fitzpatrick had been at an impasse over the matter since early April, with Bailey refusing to perform his ministerial duty of certifying the estimate adhered to proper legal form. Bailey eventually did so one day after his Supreme Court loss, but Secretary of State Jay Ashcroft burned additional time by waiting several more days before completing the final procedural steps necessary to allow the petitions to be circulated.

However, the delays continue as the petition sponsors challenge slanted ballot language Ashcroft prepared for measures claiming they would “allow for dangerous, unregulated and unrestricted abortions” and “nullify longstanding Missouri law protecting the right to life.” State law requires ballot language to be a “true and impartial statement of the purposes of the proposed measure” and not include argumentative or prejudicial language.

The sponsors filed a pre-emptive lawsuit over the ballot language weeks ago, and Cole County Circuit Judge Jon Beetem, a Republican, held a hearing on July 26. However, Beetem agreed with Ashcroft that the suit was premature since at that time of the hearing the petitions hadn’t yet been certified. Beetem plans to again take up the matter on Aug. 3 once plaintiffs re-file the case.

Time is a significant factor in the initiative petition process since supporters must gather a minimum of about 172,000 signatures from registered Missouri voters and submit them to the Secretary of State’s Office by early May to qualify for the November 2024 ballot.

Abortion rights advocates filed 11 different versions of the proposed constitutional amendment, each with slightly different wording. However, only one will be circulated for signatures. Filing multiple versions of a petition is a standard practice to dissuade frivolous legal challenges seeking to delay circulation, an opposition tactic that became common in the late 2000s.
---
|
---
| **Lawsuit set to challenge restrictions on transgender care**
Legal groups representing transgender youth, their families and medical providers are poised to file a lawsuit challenging the constitutionality of a new Missouri law making it illegal to provide gender-affirming health care to minors. Since the law is set to take effect Aug. 28, a hearing prior to that date is expected once the case is filed.

Senate Bill 49 bans gender-affirming surgery for minors, which currently only happens in rare situations. It also imposes a four-year ban on the use of puberty-blocking drugs and hormone therapy for transitioning minors, although those who started transitioning before the bill takes effect can continue treatment.

The case is being brought by Lambda Legal, an organization that defends LGBTQ rights, and the ACLU of Missouri on behalf several transgender children and their parents, as well as providers of gender-affirming medical care. Although the ACLU said in a July 25 news release that the case has been filed in Cole County Circuit Court, there was still no record of it in the state’s online case-tracking system as of the morning of July 27.

The lawsuit, according to a copy of the filing posted on the ACLU’s website, alleges SB 49 violates the equal protection, natural rights and due process clauses of the Missouri Constitution by restricting certain types of medical care for a subclass of people. It also claims SB 49 is an unconstitutional “special law” that applies to some groups but not other similarly situated groups.

The Republican-controlled General Assembly enacted SB 49, along with a separate legislation restricting participation in school sports by transgender athletes at any level, in May. Gov. Mike Parson, a Republican, quietly signed both measures into in early June. The legislation was part of national effort by GOP-controlled states to restrict the rights of transgender residents. The case challenging SB 49 is _Emily Noe, et al., v. Michael L. Parson, et al._
---
|
---
| **2023 SENATE BILLS**
[_**SB 63**_](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=f015d8f785&e=a088917f89) \- Creates new provisions relating to financial institutions

[_**SB 64**_](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=1aa2ddc55a&e=a088917f89) \- Enacts new law relating to wage range inquiries by employees and prospective employees

[**SB 65**](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=15dc2b4707&e=a088917f89) \- Modifies provisions relating to the offense of unlawful use of weapons

[_**SB 145**_](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=cdd0c559dc&e=a088917f89) \- Creates certificates of exemplary conduct and good moral character issued by circuit courts

[_**SB 146**_](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=12b8791787&e=a088917f89) \- Creates provisions relating to compensation for wrongful convictions

[_**SB 147**_](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=92f9039a8a&e=a088917f89) \- Modifies provisions relating to parole eligibility

[_**SB 215**_](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=cf2fd92fc1&e=a088917f89) \- Establishes provisions relating to alternative dispute resolution

[**_SB 216_**](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=58da96658c&e=a088917f89) \- Designates "James W Brooks Memorial Highway" in St. Louis City and St. Louis County

**[_SB 217_](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=c882c08531&e=a088917f89) **\- Creates the offense of unlawful possession by a minor

[_**SB 270**_](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=489e3a9a9a&e=a088917f89) \- Modifies the practice of dentistry to include the prescription and administration of vaccines

[**SB 363**](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=19a32f3456&e=a088917f89) \- Specifies that the St. Louis City school board shall fill any vacancy that occurs in such school board outside of the normal election cycle

[_**SJR 21**_](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=bdc2d345a8&e=a088917f89) \- Authorizes counties to freeze the real property assessed values of certain senior citizens
---

| **HELP FOR SMALL BUSINESS AND NON- PROFITS **
The U.S. Small Business Administration approved Missouri’s request for assistance through the federal agency’s disaster loan program. This move makes low-interest disaster loans available to small businesses and private non-profits severely impacted by the Coronavirus (COVID-19).

SBA’s Economic Injury Disaster Loans offer up to $2 million in assistance for each affected small business or non-profit. These loans can provide vital economic support to small businesses to help overcome the temporary loss of revenue they are experiencing.

Applicants will be able to apply online, receive additional disaster assistance information and [download applications](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=415cc9e055&e=a088917f89). Applicants may also call SBA’s Customer Service Center at (800) 659-2955 or email [disastercustomerservice@sba.gov](mailto:disastercustomerservice@sba.gov) for more information on SBA disaster assistance.

| **RX OUTREACH EXPANDS ELIGIBILITY **
Below is a memo from a trusted non-profit:

As a non-profit, mail-order pharmacy, Rx Outreach is uniquely positioned to help stop the spread of COVID-19 for people who are quarantined and those who may be afraid to travel outside of their homes to the clinic or pharmacy.

Rx Outreach lifted its income guidelines during this crisis so that **anyone, regardless of income, can participate in their program.**

Rx Outreach medication prices are generally 70-80% below retail and are transparent. Shipping is free.

---
|
---
| **LOW-COST INSULIN** Before COVID-19 insulin users were having a difficult time affording medication due to the tripling of the cost over the last decade. In the wake of COVID-19, it has become even more difficult for those who rely on insulin to afford the inflated cost. Eli Lilly and Company has introduced a program to purchase Basaglar insulin at significant cost savings. Please call Lilly Diabetes Solution Center at 833-808-1234 between 9 am and 9 pm, M-F to see if you qualify for these savings. More information can be found at the [Eli Lilly and Co. Website](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=07bbab7468&e=a088917f89).

clean_body: State officials finally allow abortion rights petition to proceed | | | | | | | Capitol Report July 30, 2023 | | | | Contact Senator Roberts 201 W. Capitol Ave., Rm 329 Jefferson City, MO 65101 (573) 751-4415 Steve.Roberts@senate.mo.gov | | | | | | | | | | | | State officials finally allow abortion rights petition to proceed State officials on July 26 finally signed off an initiative effort that seeks to put a proposed constitutional amendment protecting abortion rights on next year’s statewide ballot. The final certification of the petitions came nearly two months after the May 1 statutory deadline by which the job was supposed to have been completed due to procedural obstruction by Republican officeholders, with further delays still looming. The Missouri Supreme Court eliminated one obstacle on July 20 when it ruled Attorney General Andrew Bailey had no legal authority to try to force State Treasurer Scott Fitzpatrick to drastically inflate the estimated cost to taxpayers of enshrining abortion rights in the state constitution. Bailey and Fitzpatrick had been at an impasse over the matter since early April, with Bailey refusing to perform his ministerial duty of certifying the estimate adhered to proper legal form. Bailey eventually did so one day after his Supreme Court loss, but Secretary of State Jay Ashcroft burned additional time by waiting several more days before completing the final procedural steps necessary to allow the petitions to be circulated. However, the delays continue as the petition sponsors challenge slanted ballot language Ashcroft prepared for measures claiming they would “allow for dangerous, unregulated and unrestricted abortions” and “nullify longstanding Missouri law protecting the right to life.” State law requires ballot language to be a “true and impartial statement of the purposes of the proposed measure” and not include argumentative or prejudicial language. The sponsors filed a pre-emptive lawsuit over the ballot language weeks ago, and Cole County Circuit Judge Jon Beetem, a Republican, held a hearing on July 26. However, Beetem agreed with Ashcroft that the suit was premature since at that time of the hearing the petitions hadn’t yet been certified. Beetem plans to again take up the matter on Aug. 3 once plaintiffs re-file the case. Time is a significant factor in the initiative petition process since supporters must gather a minimum of about 172,000 signatures from registered Missouri voters and submit them to the Secretary of State’s Office by early May to qualify for the November 2024 ballot. Abortion rights advocates filed 11 different versions of the proposed constitutional amendment, each with slightly different wording. However, only one will be circulated for signatures. Filing multiple versions of a petition is a standard practice to dissuade frivolous legal challenges seeking to delay circulation, an opposition tactic that became common in the late 2000s. | | Lawsuit set to challenge restrictions on transgender care Legal groups representing transgender youth, their families and medical providers are poised to file a lawsuit challenging the constitutionality of a new Missouri law making it illegal to provide gender-affirming health care to minors. Since the law is set to take effect Aug. 28, a hearing prior to that date is expected once the case is filed. Senate Bill 49 bans gender-affirming surgery for minors, which currently only happens in rare situations. It also imposes a four-year ban on the use of puberty-blocking drugs and hormone therapy for transitioning minors, although those who started transitioning before the bill takes effect can continue treatment. The case is being brought by Lambda Legal, an organization that defends LGBTQ rights, and the ACLU of Missouri on behalf several transgender children and their parents, as well as providers of gender-affirming medical care. Although the ACLU said in a July 25 news release that the case has been filed in Cole County Circuit Court, there was still no record of it in the state’s online case-tracking system as of the morning of July 27. The lawsuit, according to a copy of the filing posted on the ACLU’s website, alleges SB 49 violates the equal protection, natural rights and due process clauses of the Missouri Constitution by restricting certain types of medical care for a subclass of people. It also claims SB 49 is an unconstitutional “special law” that applies to some groups but not other similarly situated groups. The Republican-controlled General Assembly enacted SB 49, along with a separate legislation restricting participation in school sports by transgender athletes at any level, in May. Gov. Mike Parson, a Republican, quietly signed both measures into in early June. The legislation was part of national effort by GOP-controlled states to restrict the rights of transgender residents. The case challenging SB 49 is Emily Noe, et al., v. Michael L. Parson, et al. | | 2023 SENATE BILLS \- Creates new provisions relating to financial institutions \- Enacts new law relating to wage range inquiries by employees and prospective employees \- Modifies provisions relating to the offense of unlawful use of weapons \- Creates certificates of exemplary conduct and good moral character issued by circuit courts \- Creates provisions relating to compensation for wrongful convictions \- Modifies provisions relating to parole eligibility \- Establishes provisions relating to alternative dispute resolution \- Designates "James W Brooks Memorial Highway" in St. Louis City and St. Louis County \- Creates the offense of unlawful possession by a minor \- Modifies the practice of dentistry to include the prescription and administration of vaccines \- Specifies that the St. Louis City school board shall fill any vacancy that occurs in such school board outside of the normal election cycle \- Authorizes counties to freeze the real property assessed values of certain senior citizens | | "Signed By Governor" | "Truly Agreed Bills" | | | | | HELP FOR SMALL BUSINESS AND NON- PROFITS The U.S. Small Business Administration approved Missouri’s request for assistance through the federal agency’s disaster loan program. name: Tamie
subject: I was never a Barbie girl
body:
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/67319657/419306078/1649708063?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS8xMDAwNjciLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYjA0MWNhMDQtMWMyZi1lZTExLWI4ZjAtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=XTvf5IO0koAHjft7jyMt25uExRfGVmxtakLjAhpP5pA=)
---
Friend, Last week, the classic toy doll Barbie - marketed to little girls and young women for decades was made into a massive Hollywood blockbuster that is dominating summer box offices. I’m going to go see it today, and yea, I’m wearing hot pink. While _Barbie _the movie has added a significant feminist twist, and I should note that I am _beyond excited _to see it, Barbie has not always been this way. It wasn’t until recently that Barbies were given careers, such as Veterinarian Barbie, Scientist Barbie, or even Astronaut Barbie. When I was growing up, Barbie reinforced stereotypes that told girls their proper place was in the home and being domestic: cooking, cleaning, and looking pretty. Barbie was not designed to show young girls that women could fill broader roles in society or the workplace.
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/67319658/419306079/1649708063?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=defeat-jordan-7-24-23-picture_25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS8xMDAwNjciLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYjA0MWNhMDQtMWMyZi1lZTExLWI4ZjAtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=XTvf5IO0koAHjft7jyMt25uExRfGVmxtakLjAhpP5pA=)
---
_Me when I was younger._
---
| | Even at a young age, that never sat right with me. There weren’t any career-oriented Barbies when I was little. Instead, at that age I played with my five boy cousins. We played office, and I was either the owner or manager. We rode four wheelers and played with remote control cars. Once I entered the professional stage of life, I kept true to who I was and set out to break one glass ceiling after another in male dominated industries. I grew up with boys, so I am naturally competitive with them and comfortable beating them which is why I am best suited to take out Jim Jordan. My professional drive and work ethic commonly resulted in being referred to by my male colleagues as having “balls of steel.” Although I’m sure it wasn’t really meant to be a compliment, I took this backhanded insult and wore it like a badge of honor. **Listen, I couldn’t be running for Congress at all, much less against the most vile and mean right-wing extremist in Congress, Jim Jordan, if I wasn’t prepared to overcome and rise above the sexism that comes from being a woman with the tenacity and grit to stand up, fight back, and win. **
---
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/67319659/419306080/1649708063?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=defeat-jordan-7-24-23-picture_25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS8xMDAwNjciLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYjA0MWNhMDQtMWMyZi1lZTExLWI4ZjAtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=XTvf5IO0koAHjft7jyMt25uExRfGVmxtakLjAhpP5pA=)
---
_While I don't support old school barbies I can definitely get behind a hot pink blazer!_
---
Some may frame this as an extra burden for a female candidate, but for me it is quite the opposite: these challenges are nothing new, and give me no pause. In fact, these experiences make me the most qualified candidate to take the fight to Jordan—I’ve been engaged in this kind of battle my entire life. **It may be a Barbie world, but I am NOT a Barbie girl.** And Jim Jordan won’t be able to “put me in my place.” **So, let’s take on Jim Jordan together.** [I am ready, and eager, to take the fight to Jordan and show the voters that I have what it takes to win and to represent them with energy, determination, and integrity. Can I count on you to stand with me and say enough is enough?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/67319661/419306082/1649708063?refcode=barbie&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS8xMDAwNjciLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYjA0MWNhMDQtMWMyZi1lZTExLWI4ZjAtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=XTvf5IO0koAHjft7jyMt25uExRfGVmxtakLjAhpP5pA=)

clean_body: | | | = Friend, Last week, the classic toy doll Barbie - marketed to little girls and young women for decades was made into a massive Hollywood blockbuster that is dominating summer box offices. I’m going to go see it today, and yea, I’m wearing hot pink. While Barbie the movie has added a significant feminist twist, and I should note that I am beyond excited to see it, Barbie has not always been this way. It wasn’t until recently that Barbies were given careers, such as Veterinarian Barbie, Scientist Barbie, or even Astronaut Barbie. When I was growing up, Barbie reinforced stereotypes that told girls their proper place was in the home and being domestic: cooking, cleaning, and looking pretty. Barbie was not designed to show young girls that women could fill broader roles in society or the workplace. = Me when I was younger. | | Even at a young age, that never sat right with me. There weren’t any career-oriented Barbies when I was little. Instead, at that age I played with my five boy cousins. We played office, and I was either the owner or manager. We rode four wheelers and played with remote control cars. Once I entered the professional stage of life, I kept true to who I was and set out to break one glass ceiling after another in male dominated industries. I grew up with boys, so I am naturally competitive with them and comfortable beating them which is why I am best suited to take out Jim Jordan. My professional drive and work ethic commonly resulted in being referred to by my male colleagues as having “balls of steel.” Although I’m sure it wasn’t really meant to be a compliment, I took this backhanded insult and wore it like a badge of honor. Listen, I couldn’t be running for Congress at all, much less against the most vile and mean right-wing extremist in Congress, Jim Jordan, if I wasn’t prepared to overcome and rise above the sexism that comes from being a woman with the tenacity and grit to stand up, fight back, and win. = While I don't support old school barbies I can definitely get behind a hot pink blazer! Some may frame this as an extra burden for a female candidate, but for me it is quite the opposite: these challenges are nothing new, and give me no pause. In fact, these experiences make me the most qualified candidate to take the fight to Jordan—I’ve been engaged in this kind of battle my entire life. It may be a Barbie world, but I am NOT a Barbie girl. And Jim Jordan won’t be able to “put me in my place.” So, let’s take on Jim Jordan together. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | = | | = = | = = | | Donate= or****= In Gratitude, -Tamie | = To contribute via check, please address to: 273 SARATOGA ST. DELAWARE, OH 43015 you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please | |

clean_body: | | | Team Ruiz hopes that you and your family are safe and healthy. Public health across the country is our top concern during this unprecedented time. To view the most up to date CDC COVID-19 safety recommendations, And if you have a moment, please read our message below. Folks, we’re at a crucial turning point in this campaign. Unlike our opponents, our campaign isn’t built on the backs of high-dollar donors or corporate lobbyists... And while others scrape and claw to win the endorsements of those with the deepest checkbooks and loudest voices, our campaign is busy fighting for you. We don’t have shady donor networks. We don’t run off to smoke-filled rooms to fill our campaign coffers. Our campaign is built by hard-working folks like you, chipping in $5, $10, or $15 at a time to fuel our movement. name: Friends of the Earth
subject: Your Signature is Needed: Please do not let another wolf die a cruel death
body:
| | | | | [](https://us.engagingnetworks.app/page/email/click/10012/1904656?email=RepJ2ZGQKX6xm5Aey7FwClESkN1MQhKC&campid=UI6tX83ZqbKZkArzVWMSmA==)
---|---|---

| | **Friend, innocent wolves and pups are being viciously MURDERED as you read this!** Right now, wolves are being: _**CHOKED** to death by snares,_ _Lured from their dens then**SHOT** to death, _ _**RUN over** by snowmobiles,_ _And even**DYNAMITED** in their dens!_ This is unfortunately not all, Friend. Wolves are up against various acts of violence and brutality. _So, who is responsible for these barbaric acts?_ _**CRUEL TROPHY HUNTERS.**_ And what makes this even worse... it is all _**_LEGAL._**_ Wolf packs are **being killed off in droves** meanwhile the Biden Administration is doing little to protect against the attacks to __**REMOVE**__ existing wolf protections. **[If this happens, vulnerable wolves stand little chance of survival. Please, Friend take action now to SAVE this treasured species from EXTINCTION: Add your name by 11:59pm TONIGHT before it’s too late>>](https://us.engagingnetworks.app/page/email/click/10012/1904657?email=RepJ2ZGQKX6xm5Aey7FwClESkN1MQhKC&campid=UI6tX83ZqbKZkArzVWMSmA==)** | [SIGN THE PETITION](https://us.engagingnetworks.app/page/email/click/10012/1904658?email=RepJ2ZGQKX6xm5Aey7FwClESkN1MQhKC&campid=UI6tX83ZqbKZkArzVWMSmA==)
---
While several states have made efforts to preserve and restore the fragile gray wolf population, the Northern Rockies states that border Yellowstone have **_NO PROTECTIONS_** for this iconic canine. In fact, these states continue to pass laws that make it **_EVEN EASIER_** for vicious trophy hunters to **RECKLESSLY MURDER** these precious wolves. **Now, as few as 83 wolves are reported to remain in Yellowstone.**
Wolves aren’t bound by state lines, so it’s not uncommon for them to innocently wander into states where they are in grave danger of being _**shredded to pieces**_ by **MERCILESS** trophy hunters.
These attacks on vulnerable wolf packs leave wolf pups _**hungry, afraid, and alone**_ when they are left to survive without their mother. It’s only a matter of time before these helpless pups die of _**HYPOTHERMIA**_ **_or_** _**STARVATION.**_ **[We need YOU to be the voice for our beloved canines before they no longer exist! Please take 30 seconds right now to speak up Friend>>](https://us.engagingnetworks.app/page/email/click/10012/1904659?email=RepJ2ZGQKX6xm5Aey7FwClESkN1MQhKC&campid=UI6tX83ZqbKZkArzVWMSmA==)**
[SIGN THE PETITION](https://us.engagingnetworks.app/page/email/click/10012/1904658?email=RepJ2ZGQKX6xm5Aey7FwClESkN1MQhKC&campid=UI6tX83ZqbKZkArzVWMSmA==)
---
**Thank you for protecting wolves,
Friends of the Earth**
|

clean_body: They’re coming after me, instead of the real enemy.| | | Friend, I told you about the RINO plot to sabotage us in the primary. But there’s something more I have to add... ...With all of the revelations coming out about the Biden Crime Family, why are these RINOs choosing to come after ME instead of using the power of the U.S. Senate to go after the Crook in the White House who’s bled our country dry in order to enrich himself? The reason is simple – they hate me more than they hate Crooked Joe. They’re wolves in sheep’s clothing. They’re Republicans In Name Only. Just look at what they’re doing within our primary. These RINOs, who are working on a $370 MILLION plot to sabotage us, are traitors because they’re forcing us into a two-front battle instead of uniting against Biden and his weaponized government. ...It’s our campaign that is leading with VOTERS by 40 – even 50 points! ...It’s our campaign that is funded by grassroots donors giving $1, $5, $24, and so on. ...It’s our campaign that’s greeted by unbelievable enthusiasm from hardworking people whenever I’m in town. But these special interest RINOs – who at the end of the day, are hardly any different from Biden and Soros – want to use their money to exert their will and power over the hardworking people of the nation. I will not stand for it. I ran for office to give YOU a voice in your own country again. And I will NEVER SURRENDER our mission to put the people of our country first. | | Thank you, Donald J. Trump 45th President of the United States \---------- Forwarded message --------- From: Donald J. Trump Date: Sat, Jul 22, 2023 Subject: In these moments, you learn who your true allies are... To: <dpwillis67@gmail.com> | | Friend, At a time when the Biden Department of Justice is trying to JAIL ME FOR LIFE, disloyal Republicans – or RINOs as we like to call them – are contriving a $370 MILLION PLOT to try and sabotage us in the primary. It’s during moments of crisis that you learn who your true allies are – and who would gladly stab you in the back. . . The harsh truth is that these RINO special interest donors don’t actually care if Biden gets 4 more years in the White House. The ONLY thing they fear is a president who puts the AMERICAN PEOPLE FIRST. And that makes me their public enemy number one. If they were true supporters of our America First principles, then they’d take that $370 MILLION and put it toward building a state-of-the-art vote gathering operation that would completely SWAMP the Democrats’ ballot harvesting and cheating schemes. That’s why our campaign is filling the void to build that massive operation so that we can be 100% prepared to FIRE Crooked Joe the second I’m declared the nominee. While the cowards who would stab me in the back have recently been exposed, I do know that I have 74 MILLION courageous allies who refuse to surrender our nation to tyranny. Even a contribution of just $1 alongside your fellow patriots is enough to OUTRAISE these RINOs. | | Thank you, Donald J. Hey -

I just got home from my weekly trip to the grocery store.

We are paying **WAY TOO MUCH** for basics. Bread, eggs, milk, and even toilet paper is **CRAZY** expensive right now. I'm lucky only to be buying for myself (I just graduated from college), but I can't imagine how hard it is for families.

The Biden Administration is messing with real families who need to feed their kids - and they don't care.

**_We truly are blessed in so many ways, but that doesn 't mean that Biden ROBBING us left and right can keep happening. _**

Aren't you tired of the rising prices?

**[Click here if you're sick and tired of the status quo. SOS America PAC is working hard to get Miami Mayor Francis Suarez on the GOP Presidential debate stage in August...

...but to qualify, **he needs 40,000 individual donors giving at least a dollar.**

Whether or not you support Mayor Suarez, **we think the GOP will benefit _tremendously_ from hearing from a bold, young conservative mayor who won 80% of the vote in a major city** during his reelection campaign.

Mayor Suarez has **slashed the number of homeless** people in Miami...while creating a **massive economic and population boom**...while **slashing bureaucracy** and restoring power to the people.

That’s a record and a story that **deserves to be heard by the American people** in this primary.

We’re not asking for you to cast your vote for Mayor Suarez...we’re just asking for you to **give him a fighting chance to make his case to Republican primary voters** on the debate stage.

name: Katie Porter
subject: ‘Impact’ is what meteorites do
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Senate
body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/14558?t=1&akid=27199%2E3422442%2EyFRL36)
Derek,
Impact: I despise the word.
As I tell my team, “impact” is what meteorites do. I always want to go beyond and explain *what* the effect of something is, whether that thing is legislation or your donation.
Price gouging at the gas pump, for example, has _impacted_ all of us. But for many Californians, it meant putting groceries back on shelves or not taking that family road trip. For Big Oil executives it meant record profits, and maybe even a new yacht!
When it comes to your contributions to our campaign, they don’t just impact this race.
* Donations allow us to open field offices, hire organizers, and knock doors.
* They help us run critical TV and digital ads.
* And your support proves to our opponents and special interest groups that we don’t need corporate PAC and lobbyist money to win.

This campaign simply isn’t possible without the support of grassroots donors. [**Can you chip in a few bucks today to power our campaign?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230730-2?refcode=EMAJ30_2_IMP_20230730_V1_F&refcode2=27199_3422442_yFRL36&akid=27199%2E3422442%2EyFRL36)
**[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230730-2?refcode=EMAJ30_2_IMP_20230730_V1_F&refcode2=27199_3422442_yFRL36&akid=27199%2E3422442%2EyFRL36)**
Thank YOU!
Katie Porter

Katie is running for the U.S. Senate because Californians deserve a warrior fighting for them in Washington. Our campaign is people-powered, we don’t take a dime from corporate PACs, federal lobbyists, or executives from Big Oil or Big Pharma. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-footer?refcode=EMAJ30_2_IMP_20230730_V1_F&t=2&refcode2=27199_3422442_yFRL36&akid=27199%2E3422442%2EyFRL36)**
---

## Why Chris Christie:

Chris Christie is a proven leader who fearlessly tells it like it is. As a successful governor in a blue state with a track record of real accomplishments, Christie has consistently confronted challenging issues head-on, making difficult decisions without hesitation. To ensure a conservative Republican victory in the 2024 presidential election, we deserve an open, honest debate that focuses on critical issues. Governor Christie's voice at the debates will ensure our party engages in the robust, direct, truth-telling conversation we need to start winning again. Join us in supporting Chris Christie's candidacy for president and ensuring his direct, no-nonsense agenda at the RNC-sponsored presidential debates. Your participation is vital in making this happen. Sign up today to "Tell It Like It Is" and help our party and country select a president we can all be proud of. Together, we can shape a brighter future for America.

Why? Simply: there’s a lot to do in the summer! Whether it’s finding time to go on vacation or beating the heat by catching a summer blockbuster in the movie theaters — or this year, Barbenheimer — there just isn’t a lot of attention on an election more than a year away. | [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/67325445/419310188/1246442681?refcode=EM_FL_VAS_20230730_fr_july_cm_nc_300dodon_xx_hdr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzAxMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MzYyZTI1ZS0yMTJmLWVlMTEtYjhmMC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=k6mmE0yGhu_LNTfIRQMwTNSgK2kJrDngA5YWZEAwMv8=)

Hey, everyone. It’s Sunday afternoon, so I’ll be quick. **July is one of the worst months for fundraising for nearly every single campaign.** Why? Simply: there’s a lot to do in the summer! Whether it’s finding time to go on vacation or beating the heat by catching a summer blockbuster in the movie theaters — or this year, Barbenheimer — there just isn’t a lot of attention on an election more than a year away. **But that’s exactly why we can’t let up the pressure.** _Millions_ of dollars were poured into this race last November to beat Gabe. Gabe defied the odds, beating his opponent by just 1,350 votes. ****This race is likely to be razor thin once again. And what we raise now helps us build the campaign we need to win.[Can you rush a donation before we close the books on July?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/67325446/419310189/1246442681?refcode=EM_FL_VAS_20230730_fr_july_cm_nc_300dodon_xx_l1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzAxMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MzYyZTI1ZS0yMTJmLWVlMTEtYjhmMC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=k6mmE0yGhu_LNTfIRQMwTNSgK2kJrDngA5YWZEAwMv8=)**** **** | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

Whether it’s finding time to go on vacation or beating the heat by catching a summer blockbuster in the movie theaters — or this year, Barbenheimer — there just isn’t a lot of attention on an election more than a year away. | = Hey, everyone. It’s Sunday afternoon, so I’ll be quick. July is one of the worst months for fundraising for nearly every single campaign. Why? Simply: there’s a lot to do in the summer! Whether it’s finding time to go on vacation or beating the heat by catching a summer blockbuster in the movie theaters — or this year, Barbenheimer — there just isn’t a lot of attention on an election more than a year away. But that’s exactly why we can’t let up the pressure. Millions of dollars were poured into this race last November to beat Gabe. Gabe defied the odds, beating his opponent by just 1,350 votes. **This race is likely to be razor thin once again. And what we raise now helps us build the campaign we need to win.= | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | = | = ---|---|--- = | = | = Thanks, Conor McGuire Campaign Manager ** | Gabe Vasquez is a first-generation American, former City Councilor, and conservation champion who flipped New Mexico’s 2nd Congressional District from red to blue in 2022 with the help of this people-powered movement. name: GIFFORDS PAC
subject: (bump) If you knew...
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
#

Hi there - We wanted to bump this message we sent last week. With our July fundraising deadline coming to a close at **_midnight tomorrow,**_ we need your support to help us cross the finish line.
[**So please, use this link to contribute today, and read our message below to learn just how important your contribution of $3 is to our organization.**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-july23.pfB_59&refcode=em230730-2-30&t=1&akid=6235%2E3935704%2EZcxR50)
**[CONTRIBUTE](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-july23.pfB_59&refcode=em230730-2-30&t=2&akid=6235%2E3935704%2EZcxR50)**
Thanks,
The GIFFORDS Team
\- - - - - - - - - -Original Message from July 25, 2023- - - - - - - - - - -

Friends -
If you knew that your $3 could help get the next major gun safety law passed, [**wouldn’t you donate it right away?**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-july23.pfB_59&refcode=em230730-2-30&t=3&akid=6235%2E3935704%2EZcxR50)
If you knew that your $3 went directly towards organizing, lobbying, education, advertising, research, and supporting gun safety candidates—all with a mission to end our nation’s gun violence epidemic—[**wouldn’t you chip in $3 right away?**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-july23.pfB_59&refcode=em230730-2-30&t=4&akid=6235%2E3935704%2EZcxR50)
If you knew that your $3 had the power to save someone’s life, [**wouldn’t it be the best $3 you could ever spend?**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-july23.pfB_59&refcode=em230730-2-30&t=5&akid=6235%2E3935704%2EZcxR50)
We know that may sound like an exaggeration, but it’s the truth. People are alive today because of the lifesaving laws that GIFFORDS has helped pass in states across the country.
But obviously, there’s still far more to be done. Over the weekend, our country surpassed 400 mass shootings so far this year. This is unacceptable and preventable, but we need you with us to keep moving forward in the fight to end gun violence.
[**So please, contribute $3 today. Your support directly fuels our work toward a future free from gun violence.**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-july23.pfB_59&refcode=em230730-2-30&t=6&akid=6235%2E3935704%2EZcxR50)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_
**[CONTRIBUTE $3](https://go.giffords.org/go/17015?c0=giffords-em-july23.np0YAl&refcode=em230730-2-30&amount=3&t=7&akid=6235%2E3935704%2EZcxR50)**
Thank you for all that you do,
The GIFFORDS Team

Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. www.Giffords.org
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com . We try to send only the most important information and opportunities to participate via email, but click [here](https://go.giffords.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=8&akid=6235%2E3935704%2EZcxR50) if you'd like to unsubscribe. Giffords PAC was started to support elected officials who step up to fight the gun violence epidemic. Thank you for standing with Gabby and the Giffords team.
